name: Weighted Crunch
resistance_profile: Loaded spinal flexion; hold a plate or dumbbell to progress.
cues: Flex the spine, ribs toward pelvis
execution_steps: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Hold a plate or dumbbell against your chest.
Crunch up by flexing your spine, curling your ribs toward your pelvis.
Squeeze the abs at the top, then lower slowly under control.
good_when: You want to add resistance to crunches for ab growth without a cable machine.
You've outgrown bodyweight crunches and need progressive load.
You want a simple, low-skill ab isolation.
bad_when: You have a cable available and want smoother constant tension (use a cable crunch).
You pull with your hip flexors instead of crunching the spine.
You want anti-rotation or bracing work (use a plank or Pallof press).
